controls.name: Multi-Factor Authentication
controls.description: Implement multi-factor authentication for all ECU interfaces and diagnostic access points
controls.implementationGuidance: Use hardware security modules (HSM) for key storage and implement challenge-response authentication protocols

controls.name: End-to-End Encryption
controls.description: Encrypt all communication channels using strong cryptographic algorithms (AES-256, TLS 1.3)
controls.implementationGuidance: Implement TLS 1.3 for network communications and AES-256 for data at rest. Use authenticated encryption modes (GCM)

controls.name: Secure Boot
controls.description: Implement secure boot chain with cryptographic verification of all firmware components
controls.implementationGuidance: Use hardware root of trust (e.g., TPM) and verify digital signatures at each boot stage

controls.name: Code Signing for OTA Updates
controls.description: Digitally sign all OTA update packages and verify signatures before installation
controls.implementationGuidance: Use RSA-4096 or ECDSA P-384 for signing. Implement rollback protection and version checking

controls.name: Network Segmentation
controls.description: Segment vehicle networks with firewalls between critical and non-critical domains
controls.implementationGuidance: Create separate network zones: Safety-critical, Infotainment, External connectivity. Use gateway with packet filtering

controls.name: CAN Message Authentication
controls.description: Implement message authentication codes (MAC) for CAN bus messages
controls.implementationGuidance: Use CAN-FD with authentication (e.g., AUTOSAR SecOC). Implement message counters and freshness values

controls.name: Security Event Logging
controls.description: Log all security-relevant events with tamper-proof storage
controls.implementationGuidance: Log authentication attempts, diagnostic access, firmware updates, and anomalous network activity. Use write-once storage

controls.name: Intrusion Detection System
controls.description: Implement IDS to detect anomalous behavior on vehicle networks
controls.implementationGuidance: Monitor CAN bus traffic patterns, message frequencies, and unusual ECU behavior. Use ML-based anomaly detection

controls.name: Physical Tamper Detection
controls.description: Implement tamper-evident seals and detection circuits for critical components
controls.implementationGuidance: Use tamper-evident enclosures for ECUs. Implement zeroization of keys upon tamper detection

controls.name: Diagnostic Port Access Control
controls.description: Implement authentication for diagnostic port access with lockout mechanisms
controls.implementationGuidance: Require seed-key authentication for UDS services. Implement progressive delays after failed attempts

controls.name: Incident Response Plan
controls.description: Establish procedures for detecting, responding to, and recovering from security incidents
controls.implementationGuidance: Define roles, communication channels, escalation procedures. Include field remediation and recall procedures

controls.name: Vulnerability Management Program
controls.description: Establish continuous vulnerability scanning and patch management process
controls.implementationGuidance: Monitor CVE databases, conduct regular penetration tests, prioritize patches based on CVSS scores

controls.name: Supplier Security Requirements
controls.description: Define and enforce cybersecurity requirements for all suppliers
controls.implementationGuidance: Include security clauses in contracts. Require SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) from suppliers

controls.name: Security Testing and Validation
controls.description: Conduct comprehensive security testing including fuzzing, penetration testing, and code review
controls.implementationGuidance: Use OWASP testing guidelines. Conduct third-party security assessments. Automate security testing in CI/CD

controls.name: Security Training and Awareness
controls.description: Provide regular cybersecurity training for development and operations teams
controls.implementationGuidance: Include secure coding practices, threat modeling, incident response. Conduct annual refresher training

controlCategories.Access Control: Controls related to authentication and authorization
controlCategories.System Integrity: Controls ensuring software and firmware integrity
controlCategories.Audit and Monitoring: Controls for logging and anomaly detection
